name: Punta Entinas-Sabinar Nature Reserve Loop
name_original: Salinas de Cerrillos — circular desde La Mojonera — Reserva Natural Punta Entinas-Sabinar
description: This 18.3-mile (29.4 km) easy touring bicycle route through Paraje Natural Punta Entinas-Sabinar offers diverse terrain and rich birdlife.
region_intro: Touring cycling around La Mojonera offers diverse landscapes, from the flat coastal plains of Campo de Dalías to the foothills of the Sierra de Gádor. This region in Almería, Andalucía, provides a variety of routes suitable for different skill levels. Cyclists can explore protected natural spaces like Punta Entinas-Sabinar Natural Park, which features dedicated bike lanes and unique ecological experiences. The area combines coastal rides with opportunities for more challenging ascents into the nearby mountain ranges. Best touring cycling routes around La Mojonera The most popular touring cycling route is Salinas de Cerrillos — circular from La Mojonera — Punta Entinas-Sabinar Nature Reserve, an easy 18.3 miles (29.4 km) trail that takes about 1 hour 54 minutes to complete, winding through coastal salt flats and natural parkland. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Alcazaba of Almeria – Aguadulce Marina loop from Venta de Viso (Mojonera), a difficult 56.3 miles (90.6 km) path. This route features significant elevation changes and connects coastal areas with the historic Alcazaba of Almeria. Local touring cyclists also love the Santa Ana Castle – Cerrillos Tower loop from Las Marinas, a 19.3 miles (31.1 km) trail leading through coastal areas with historical towers, often completed in about 2 hours 6 minutes. Touring cycling around La Mojonera is defined by coastal plains, the foothills of the Sierra de Gádor, and protected natural parks. The network offers options for leisurely rides along the coast and more demanding routes with elevation gain. The routes in La Mojonera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 600 reviews. More than 4000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore La Mojonera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around La Mojonera?
A: There are over 100 touring cycling routes in and around La Mojonera, offering a wide variety of experiences for cyclists of all levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when touring cycling in La Mojonera?
A: La Mojonera offers a diverse range of terrain. You can find flat coastal routes along the Campo de Dalías and through natural parks, as well as more challenging ascents into the foothills of the Sierra de Gádor. This blend allows for both leisurely rides and more demanding climbs.

Q: Are there options for both easy and challenging touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, the area caters to all skill levels. Approximately 47 routes are rated as easy, perfect for a relaxed ride, while 31 routes are considered difficult, offering significant elevation changes for experienced touring cyclists.

Q: What natural attractions can I explore while cycling in La Mojonera?
A: Many routes pass through or near beautiful natural areas. You can cycle through the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1501230" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Punta Entinas-Sabinar Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, known for its unique ecological features and birdlife, or alongside the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1383204" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salinas and Wetlands of Roquetas de Mar&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1401503" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Faro Beach and Roquetas de Mar Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt; also offer scenic coastal views.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, you can incorporate cultural stops into your rides. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5572844" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Ana Castle&lt;/a&gt; is a notable historical landmark accessible on some coastal routes. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5397690" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vicar Street Art and Murals&lt;/a&gt; offer an interesting modern cultural experience for touring cyclists.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in La Mojonera?
A: The touring cycling routes in La Mojonera are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.4 out of 5 stars from over 600 reviews. More than 4,000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes and well-maintained paths.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular easy circular route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1054922773/salinas-de-cerrillos-circular-from-la-mojonera-punta-entinas-sabinar-nature-reserve" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salinas de Cerrillos — circular from La Mojonera — Punta Entinas-Sabinar Nature Reserve&lt;/a&gt;. For a moderate option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31888057" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Ana Castle – Cerrillos Tower loop from Las Marinas&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find routes suitable for families or beginners?
A: Absolutely. La Mojonera offers numerous easy routes ideal for families or beginners. These often feature flat sections along the coast or through natural parks, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40329455" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Ana Castle – Playa de Roquetas de Mar loop from Las Marinas&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 23.4 km ride.

Q: Are there longer touring routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Experienced cyclists seeking longer distances and significant elevation gain will find challenging options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38187497" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcazaba of Almeria – Aguadulce Marina loop from Venta de Viso (Mojonera)&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 90.6 km route with substantial climbing, connecting coastal areas with the historic Alcazaba.

Q: Where can I find coastal touring cycling routes with sea views?
A: The coastal plain of Campo de Dalías provides excellent opportunities for touring cycling with sea views. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40326430" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerrillos Tower – Almerimar Tower loop from Las Marinas&lt;/a&gt; offer extensive stretches along the coastline, allowing you to enjoy the Mediterranean scenery.

Q: Is it possible to combine cycling with exploring the local wildlife?
A: Yes, especially within the Punta Entinas-Sabinar Natural Park and the Roquetas de Mar Salt Flats and Wetlands. These areas have dedicated bike lanes and platforms, providing unique opportunities for cyclists to observe diverse birdlife and other local fauna while riding.

Q: Are there any hidden gem routes for touring cyclists in La Mojonera?
A: Komoot identifies 20 'hidden gems' for hiking and cycling around La Mojonera, suggesting many localized and interesting paths beyond the most popular ones. These often offer unique perspectives of the region's diverse landscapes, from coastal paths to inland agricultural areas.
tips: The detour to the former saltworks is also worthwhile in terms of the landscape. We ended up here by chance because we got lost ...
At the exit of the town of Las Norias de Daza towards La Mojonera is this road with rafts on both sides, the Raft of the Sapo and the Raft of the Woman.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is officially rated as easy, but it offers a varied experience. While sections through the Salinas de Cerrillos are generally flat and easy, the terrain becomes rougher as you venture into the Punta Entinas-Sabinar Nature Reserve. Be prepared for sand dunes, salt marshes, and potentially challenging, non-rideable sections, especially a 'rocky trail' segment where you might need to dismount or even carry your bike. It's best suited for touring cyclists prepared for mixed conditions.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You can expect a diverse and beautiful landscape. The route features quiet beach areas, extensive sand dunes, salt marshes, and wetlands. You'll also see remnants of old saltworks, the historic Cerrillos watchtower, and glimpses of juniper and mastic forests. The coastal views are particularly picturesque.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I see on this route?
faqs.answer: The Punta Entinas-Sabinar Nature Reserve is a significant habitat for diverse wildlife, especially birds. The wetlands and salt marshes are crucial resting points for many migratory species, including flamingos. It's an excellent spot for birdwatching.

faqs.question: Are there any specific points of interest or landmarks?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes by several notable points. You'll cycle through the Salinas de Cerrillos, which are historic saltworks. You'll also find the Path Between the Balsa del Sapo and the Balsa de la Mujer, and the historic Cerrillos watchtower. The entire Punta Entinas-Sabinar Nature Reserve itself is a highlight, known for its unique ecosystem.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: Since this is a circular route starting from La Mojonera, you would typically look for parking within La Mojonera itself. Specific parking areas for the nature reserve or trailheads might be available, but general street parking in La Mojonera would be your starting point.

faqs.question: Is this route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the nature reserve is a natural area, specific regulations regarding dogs on trails, especially within protected areas like Punta Entinas-Sabinar, can vary. It's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all visitors. Check local signage for any specific restrictions.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is generally during the cooler months, such as spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these periods, the weather is more pleasant for cycling, and you can avoid the intense heat of the summer. It's also a great time for birdwatching.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for cycling in Punta Entinas-Sabinar Nature Reserve?
faqs.answer: As a natural park, the Punta Entinas-Sabinar Nature Reserve generally does not require permits or charge entrance fees for access to its trails for activities like cycling. However, always respect local regulations and signage within the protected area.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: While parts of the route through Salinas de Cerrillos are easy and flat, the sections within the Punta Entinas-Sabinar Nature Reserve can be quite rough with sand and rocky trails. This makes the full circular route potentially challenging for beginners or families with young children, especially on touring bikes. Consider the varied terrain before embarking on the full loop with less experienced riders.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several local paths. You will cycle along parts of the Camino de Roquetas, Camino Las Norias - La Mojonera, Camino de Tierras Blancas, Camino La Molina, Camino Aljibe de las Cadenas, and Calle San Sebastián Elcano.

faqs.question: What should I consider when choosing a bike for this route?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain, which includes both paved sections and rough, sandy, or rocky paths within the nature reserve, a touring bicycle might find some parts challenging. If you plan to tackle the entire route, especially the rougher sections of Punta Entinas-Sabinar, a mountain bike or a hybrid bike with wider tires would offer a more comfortable and capable experience.

waypoints.name: Path Between the Balsa del Sapo and the Balsa de la Mujer
